qa_pairs.question: When logging into a cluster, how does a YubiKey facilitate multifactor authentication?
qa_pairs.answer: When prompted for a one-time password (OTP) or a "passcode" during cluster login, a user can simply touch a button on their YubiKey. The YubiKey then automatically generates a string of characters, effectively "typing" it in to complete the authentication process without requiring any manual input from the keyboard.

qa_pairs.question: Is manual typing required when using a YubiKey for multifactor authentication on a cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: No, manual typing is not required when using a YubiKey for multifactor authentication. When a user is prompted for a one-time password (OTP) or "passcode," they simply touch a button on their YubiKey, which then automatically generates and inputs the necessary string of characters.
